title: NOC 62022 – Accommodation, Travel, Tourism and Related Services Supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 62022 captures the leadership of Canada’s vibrant travel and tourism sector. From front-desk supervisors at major resorts to travel agency leads and tour operators, these TEER 2 supervisors ensure the seamless delivery of the "Canadian Experience." As a prioritized occupation for various regional immigration pilots, this code offers a clear and diverse set of pathways for international professionals with high-level customer service and organizational skills.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group620 – Retail sales and service supervisorsMinor Group6202 – Service supervisorsNOC Code62022

duties: Coordinate and supervise the activities of hotel clerks, travel agents, and tour guides.
Establish work schedules and procedures and coordinate with other departments.
Resolve customer complaints and handle specialized guest requests.
Train staff in job duties, safety procedures, and company policies.
Requisition materials and supplies and ensure equipment is maintained.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma or vocational training in tourism or hospitality is often required.
Several years of experience in the specific service area are mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Favourable. The expansion of eco-tourism and adventure travel in Canada is expected to create significant new supervisor roles over the next decade.
labour_market_demand: Tourism is one of Canada's fastest-growing sectors post-2024. Demand is exceptionally high in British Columbia, Ontario, and Quebec. Employers are searching for supervisors who are multilingual and tech-savvy, capable of managing complex booking systems and diverse international clientele.
Global Hub: Work in world-famous destinations like Banff, Niagara Falls, and Whistler.
Industry Perks: Access to travel and accommodation benefits unique to the sector.
Diversity: Work with a truly international team and customer base.
pathways: BC PNP Skills Immigration: Specialized stream for tourism professionals.
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): Competitive for those with high language proficiency.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Addressing tourism needs in participating communities.
